commit.message:
docs: add pr-comments skill (#211)

## Summary
- Add `pr-comments` skill entry to the Skills table in README.md
- Add `jq` to compatibility requirements in pr-comments skill
- Replace Unicode arrow with ASCII `->` in js-deps update-workflow
- Use `dirname` subshell for worktree path construction in js-deps
- Use subshell for `cd` in corrupted lockfile fix to preserve cwd
- Add range comment fields (`start_line`, `side`, `position`, etc.) to
`--jq` projection
- Clarify filtering prose: top-level comment selection happens after
fetch
- Use GraphQL variables (`-f owner`, `-f name`, `-F number`) instead of
hardcoded strings
- Fix reply endpoint URL: must include PR number in path
(`/pulls/{number}/comments/{id}/replies`)

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify the README Skills table renders correctly on GitHub

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: UPM-compatible publishing for GitHub registry (#12)

* feat: keep unscoped name in package.json for GitHub (UPM compatibility)

Unity Package Manager cannot resolve scoped names (@owner/com.foo.bar).
GitHub infers ownership from the repository field and publishConfig.registry,
so the bare UPM name (com.foo.bar) can be used in package.json.

Changes:
- _update_package_json: skip _compute_scoped_name for GitHub registry
- publish_package: log unscoped display_name for GitHub
- check_package_exists: query GitHub with unscoped name
- tests: 13 new tests covering GitHub/npmjs name-handling behaviour

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pass --scope=@owner to npm publish for GitHub routing

Without the scope flag, GitHub's registry returns 404 because it cannot
determine the owning user/org from an unscoped package name alone.

publishConfig + repository fields are not sufficient — npm must be told
the scope explicitly at publish time. The name in package.json remains
unscoped so UPM can resolve it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: publish to GitHub Packages via direct HTTP PUT (bypass npm CLI)

- Add _github_publish_direct() that builds packument with scoped name
  (@owner/com.foo.bar) and PUTs directly to npm.pkg.github.com, while
  keeping unscoped name in the tarball's package.json for UPM compat
- Add _PublishConflict internal exception for HTTP 409 responses
- Route GitHub registry through direct HTTP path; all other registries
  still use npm CLI
- Remove publishConfig from package.json for GitHub (no longer needed)
- Add TestGithubPublishDirect covering scoped URL, packument body, and
  409 conflict handling (113 tests total, all passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use scoped attachment key in GitHub Packages packument

GitHub's npm registry matches attachments by key format
`@owner/name-version.tgz` (the full scoped name). Using the
unscoped key `name-version.tgz` results in a 400 'no attachments
present in packument' error.

Update _github_publish_direct to derive the attachment key as
`{scoped_name}-{version}.tgz` and add a test assertion to lock
in this behaviour.

Validated: google-protobuf v31.1.0 published successfully to
https://npm.pkg.github.com/@klumhru/com.klumhru.wrapper.google-protobuf

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: strip leading v from version in generate_package_json

UPM requires strict semver (31.1.0) without a v prefix.
Config refs use git tag-style versions (v31.1.0), so strip
any leading v before writing to package.json.

Update docs with correct UPM registry URL (must include owner
scope path /@klumhru) and .upmconfig.toml auth key guidance.

Add TestGeneratePackageJsonVersion tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add GitHub Pages static npm registry for UPM compatibility

GitHub Packages always returns scoped names (@owner/pkg) in packument
responses, causing UPM to fail with 'Expected to find progress reporting
for @owner/pkg. No packages loaded.'

Solution: after each GitHub Packages publish, generate a static
packument JSON in dist/registry/ with the unscoped name. The CI
workflow deploys this directory to GitHub Pages via actions/deploy-pages.
UPM consumers point their scoped registry at the GitHub Pages URL
(https://klumhru.github.io/package-wrappers-unity); tarballs are still
downloaded from GitHub Packages (auth via ~/.upmconfig.toml for
https://npm.pkg.github.com).

Changes:
- Add PagesPublisher class (pages_publisher.py) with update_registry()
- Call PagesPublisher after successful and conflicting GitHub publishes
  so the static registry is always kept up to date
- Add pages:write + id-token:write perms and deploy-pages steps to CI
- Update docs with correct consumer configuration
- 130 tests pass, pages_publisher.py at 100% coverage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

fix: normalize paths in WASM resource cache lookup

The tiled library requests resources with raw relative paths like
'maps/../tilesets/collision.tsx', but the pre-loading cache stores
normalized paths like 'tilesets/collision.tsx'. Added normalize_path()
to resolve '..' components before cache lookup.

commit.message:
Fix navbar dropdowns: relax CSP to allow Semantic UI inline styles

The default InvenioRDM CSP (default-src 'self') blocks the inline
styles and eval() that Semantic UI uses to show/hide dropdown menus.
This caused the + (upload) and user account navbar buttons to appear
but do nothing on click.

Added explicit content_security_policy to APP_DEFAULT_SECURE_HEADERS
with 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' — appropriate for a PoC HTTP
deployment. Harden with nonce-based CSP before going to production.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix user guide design issues

Fixed multiple design issues:
- Dark code boxes now wrap text instead of extending beyond boundaries (max-width: 100%, word-wrap, white-space: pre-wrap)
- Launch agents box in welcome section now uses dark theme with proper text contrast (rgba dark bg, light text colors)
- Quote boxes changed from light to dark theme for better readability (#1E293B bg, #CBD5E1 text)
- Typography hierarchy: H1 now 40px/800, H2 now 24px/700, welcome H2 32px/800
- Minimum font sizes: all text now 14px+ (nav-num, badges, phase-label, agent-pill)
- Text contrast improved: changed #334155 to #64748B and #475569 for better readability
- Added GitHub icon before AI Agents text in launch box to clarify which desktop icon to click

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 2: Amateur astronomy expansion — 8 new namespaces/classes

New namespace: AstroCalc.Optics
- Telescope: magnification, exit pupil, FOV, Dawes/Rayleigh limits,
  limiting magnitude, light-gathering power
- VisualMagnitude: Pogson's law, combined magnitudes, airmass (Kasten-Young),
  atmospheric extinction, surface brightness, Bortle scale

New namespace: AstroCalc.Observation
- RiseTransitSet: generalized rise/transit/set for any object (Meeus Ch.15)
- ObservingWindow: dark sky planning, Moon interference, sky quality rating

Expanded: AstroCalc.SolarSystem
- JovianMoons: Galilean satellite positions (Meeus Ch.44)
- SaturnRings: ring tilt, position angle, dimensions (Meeus Ch.45)
- PlanetaryMagnitude: visual magnitudes (Meeus Ch.41, Mallama & Hilton 2018)
- CometPosition: parabolic/elliptical/hyperbolic orbits (Meeus Ch.33-34)

Expanded: AstroCalc.Phenomena
- MoonPhaseInfo: phase name, age, illumination, next phase dates

Expanded: AstroCalc.Time
- Easter: Gregorian Easter date computation (Meeus Ch.9)

127 tests, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: server now downloads OSM tiles on-demand when peer requests missing tiles

Root cause: server had 2M tiles at OSM_CACHE_VERSION 7. After v0.1.49 bumped
to version 8 (WaterwayLine struct change), cache.load() version-checked all
tiles as stale and returned NotFound (❌) for every P2P request. Server was
queuing SRTM tiles for on-demand download but NOT OSM tiles.

Changes:
- AppState: add osm_priority_tx channel (same pattern as srtm_priority_tx)
- Tile request handler: when OsmTile returns NotFound, push (s,w,n,e) to
  osm_priority_tx channel so it gets downloaded from Overpass in background
- download_osm_on_demand_task: new async task, downloads from all 3 Overpass
  mirrors, caches as v8, announces to DHT; deduplicates in-flight requests
- GET /api/diag/osm endpoint: shows total tiles, v8-ready count, stale v7 count,
  optional ?s=lat&w=lon to check a specific tile

Also copies metaworld_alpha binary (no changes, just keeping in sync).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix auto-fix mutex: exclude non-coding agent draft PRs (#169)

* Fix auto-fix mutex: exclude all non-coding agent draft PRs

The mutex only excluded 'Daily self-assessment' and 'Daily project
assessment' title patterns, but non-coding agents (project, product)
produce draft PRs with titles like 'Project assessment YYYY-MM-DD: ...'
and 'Product analysis YYYY-MM-DD: ...'. These stale PRs blocked
the coder agent from picking up issue #168.

Broadened the exclusion regex to cover all known non-coder agent
PR title patterns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Anchor exclusion regex with ^ to prevent substring false matches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Align PROJECT-AGENT.md diagram with broadened exclusion patterns

- Use 'non-coding agent PRs' consistently instead of 'non-coder'
- Step 2 now lists all excluded title patterns, not just 'project assess'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: merge main branch conflict resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Workspace keyboard navigation and touch support

Keyboard:
- Arrow Up/Down to move selection through tree rows
- Arrow Right to expand folder, Arrow Left to collapse
- Enter to toggle folder or open file in editor
- Delete/Backspace to delete selected file (with confirm)
- Escape to clear selection
- Tree list is focusable (tabIndex=0) with focus-visible ring

commit.message:
network: Fix persistent reconnection to offline peers

Three-part fix for the system endlessly retrying dead peers:

1. list_by_tier() now filters for is_active — PHASE 1 startup no
   longer dials inactive masternodes that will just timeout.

2. PHASE 3 eviction check moved BEFORE AI cooldown check. Previously
   the failure count was only inspected when the AI said 'don't attempt'
   (during cooldown), so eviction never triggered when cooldown expired.
   Also lowered FORGET_THRESHOLD from 10 to 5 consecutive failures.

3. PeerManager tracks evicted IPs with a 1-hour cooldown. Prevents
   PeerExchange gossip from immediately re-adding a just-evicted peer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Assess and add benchmarking against LBM (#28)

* Add lbm feature and associated dependencies

- Introduced a new `lbm` feature in `pixi.toml` with a corresponding test command for benchmarking.
- Added `xlb` as a dependency for the `lbm` feature in both `pixi.toml` and `pyproject.toml`.

* Add XLB benchmark functionality and tests for segmented volume analysis

* Add synthetic porous-volume benchmark notebook against XLB

This notebook generates synthetic segmented spanning volumes with voids, solves them using XLB, extracts pore networks with snow2, and compares permeability estimates between the direct-image voxel-scale LBM reference and the extracted-network PNM workflow. The implementation includes detailed case specifications, benchmarking, and visualizations for analysis.

* Enhance documentation for XLB benchmarking and update examples to include direct-image LBM transport

* Add comprehensive tests for XLB direct solver and helper functions

* Update ipynb 13 results

* Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix XLB pressure BC masking and stub module registration in tests (#29)

* Initial plan

* Fix XLB stub module registration and inlet/outlet mask void intersection

- tests/test_benchmark_xlb.py: register xlb.operator intermediate package
  stub (with __path__) in sys.modules so that from-imports on submodules
  like xlb.operator.boundary_condition are deterministic
- src/voids/benchmarks/xlb.py: intersect inlet_mask and outlet_mask with
  aligned_void before applying pressure BCs so solid voxels on inlet/outlet
  planes are never assigned a pressure boundary condition

Co-authored-by: volpatto <5941226+volpatto@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/voids/benchmarks/xlb.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add regression test for inlet/outlet mask solid-voxel exclusion

Add test_xlb_inlet_outlet_masks_exclude_solid_voxels which creates a 2D
volume with known solid cells on the inlet and outlet planes and
inlet_outlet_buffer_cells=0, then inspects the captured BC indices to
verify:
- solid voxels are NOT assigned a pressure BC
- solid voxels ARE assigned a bounce-back BC
- interior void voxels on those planes still receive pressure BCs

Co-authored-by: volpatto <5941226+volpatto@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reorder AI provider priority for optimal performance

New priority (best → worst):
1. Groq (PRIMARY) - Fast, generous free tier ⚡
2. OpenRouter (FALLBACK) - Multiple free models 🔄
3. Gemini (BACKUP) - Paid backup once quota resets 🆘

Why this order:
- Groq: Most stable free tier, fastest inference
- OpenRouter: Multiple model options as fallback
- Gemini: Only use after quota recovery (24h)

Fix nginx 403 on static assets: set home dir to o+x

nginx runs as www-data and needs execute permission on every
directory in the path to /static. Without chmod o+x on the home
directory the entire /static location returns 403 Forbidden,
leaving the UI with bare HTML and no CSS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Upload conflict handling: backend returns 409 when file exists,
  frontend catches it, prompts confirm, retries with overwrite=1
- Per-folder upload buttons: each folder row shows an upload icon
  on hover, triggers file input scoped to that directory
- Paste-to-upload: compose box extracts images from clipboard paste
  events and adds them as media attachments
- Resource link URL sanitization: validate href against safe schemes
- 3 new tests for upload overwrite/conflict handling (344 total)

commit.message:
feat(hardening): round 6 — 10 weaknesses fixed with TDD

W1: /load-script path containment check + symlink resolution (RCE prevention)
W2: Dashboard eval-file resolves symlinks before containment check
W3: Replace Task.Wait() with do! in SSE snapshot replay (thread pool starvation)
W4: 4MB body size cap in readJsonProp/readJsonBody (OOM prevention)
W5: ManifestPersistence payloadEnd uses List.min (correct multi-section boundary)
W6: StandbySession.BaseUrl threaded through StandbyReady — WorkerBaseUrl populated after hot-swap
W7: getWarmupContextForElm uses 5s linked CancellationToken (was 100s default)
W8: DaemonSessionStopped event only written inside Ok branch of StopSession
W9: proxyPost gates body read on ContentLength <= 1MB (OOM proxy prevention)
W10: FeaturePushState.KnownBindings maintained incrementally in recordEval (O(1) vs O(n))

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite index.html with universe portfolio layout

Replace the existing index.html with a complete galaxy-themed
interactive portfolio featuring planet navigation, section panels
(home, about, skills, projects, experience, contact), loading screen,
code viewer modal, zoom controls, and bottom nav menu.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 8.6: Package Rust library build as go generate step

Implements ROADMAP.md Phase 8.6 - enables 'go generate && go build' workflow
for building the full static binary without requiring Make.

Changes:
- Created internal/render/generate.go with go:generate directive
- Added scripts/build-rust.sh comprehensive build automation script
  * Auto-detects host architecture and musl Rust target
  * Checks for required tools (musl-gcc, cargo, rustup)
  * Auto-installs musl Rust target if missing
  * Builds Rust static library (librender.a)
  * Compiles musl compatibility stub (dl_find_object_stub.o)
- Updated README.md with go generate workflow documentation
- Updated ROADMAP.md to mark Phase 8.6 as complete
- Added scripts/README.md with comprehensive documentation

Workflow:
1. go generate ./...  # builds Rust artifacts
2. go build ./...     # builds Go binary with CGO linking

Integration:
- Fully compatible with existing Makefile workflow
- Zero regressions in code complexity metrics
- All tests passing (except pre-existing GPU hardware tests)
- Static binary verified (ldd reports 'not a dynamic executable')

Benefits:
- Enables Go-native build pipelines
- Simplifies CI/CD integration for teams using go tooling
- Maintains full compatibility with make build
- Clear error messages for missing dependencies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
